jd_full_text:
Learning & Development Specialist — University of Washington. Location: Seattle, WA. Salary Range: $80,172 - $104,568 annually. Position Type: Regular, Full-Time (100% FTE). Req: REQ-0000133162.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: The role focuses on developing and delivering training programs (approx. 30% of time), serving as primary instructor for the 'Stepping Stones to Success' leadership program (approx. 15%), and managing training logistics. The position develops, delivers, and tracks training for the ~1,100 employees of UW Facilities. Additional duties include supervising/mentoring 1-2 interns, coordinating with vendors, and using data to evaluate training effectiveness.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor's Degree and a minimum of 3-4 years of progressively responsible experience in training, communication, program coordination or management positions.

ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS: Demonstrated curriculum design and delivery experience; public speaking ability to diverse audiences; intermediate to advanced skills in MS Office applications; strong communication and conflict management skills; ability to work independently and as part of a team; critical thinking and problem-solving capabilities.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: Learning Management System experience; familiarity with adult learning principles (Andragogy, ADDIE); 1-2 years supervising or mentoring staff/interns.

SOURCE NOTE: UW Workday posting blocked direct fetch; JD retrieved via LinkedIn listing (job ID 4422651407) for this requisition plus orchestrator-supplied summary. No requirements fabricated.
fit: GO — Cap-exempt public university (sponsors); asks Bachelor's + 3-4yr in training/communication/program coordination, which Jamie's ~3yr InGenius enablement (needs assessments, 600+ participants, 78% enrollment, SOPs/guides) maps to directly; LMS + ADDIE + adult-learning + mentoring all covered. URGENT: posting closes June 17, 2026 (2 days) — prioritize submission.
